documents.title: Agent run state machine
documents.content: The agent run state machine advances mock agent work through pending, waiting for approval, completed, failed, or cancelled states. Concurrent advance requests are serialized by locking the run row.

documents.title: Approval gate policy
documents.content: The approval gate policy requires a simulated actor role for approve or reject actions, records who resolved the gate, and stops the run when an approval expires.

documents.title: Safe execution policy
documents.content: The safe execution policy classifies proposed actions before execution. Destructive filesystem actions, dependency installation, migrations, and external adapter calls require explicit approval or remain blocked in the mock runtime.

documents.title: Retention policy summary
documents.content: The retention policy summary says oversized documents are rejected, soft-deleted context is excluded from search, and quota or deletion operations write audit events.

documents.title: Context source lifecycle
documents.content: The context source lifecycle covers source creation, duplicate document checks, chunk persistence, mock embedding generation, soft delete, restore, and active retrieval exclusion for deleted chunks.

queries.query: how are concurrent agent run advances serialized
what happens when an approval expires
expectedChunkContains: stops the run when an approval expires

queries.query: which policy blocks destructive filesystem actions
what context operations write audit events
expectedChunkContains: quota or deletion operations write audit events
